prompt: Draft a detailed isothermal titration calorimetry (ITC) protocol for measuring the binding thermodynamics (KD, ΔH, ΔS, stoichiometry) of a nucleotide analogue inhibitor to the dengue virus NS5 RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp) domain using a MicroCal PEAQ-ITC. The protocol should include: (1) protein preparation: NS5-RdRp domain (residues 272–900) in ITC buffer (20 mM HEPES pH 7.5, 150 mM NaCl, 2 mM MgCl2, 5% glycerol), concentration 20 µM in cell, (2) inhibitor preparation: 200 µM in identical buffer, (3) ITC run parameters: 25°C, 19 injections of 2 µL, 150 s spacing, (4) reference cell: identical buffer without protein, (5) data analysis: one-site binding model in MicroCal PEAQ-ITC Analysis, heat of dilution subtraction, (6) controls: DMSO vehicle control at equivalent concentration. Include troubleshooting for aggregation and low enthalpy signals.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and ITC methodology literature; target: dengue NS5 RdRp; organism: Dengue virus serotype 2; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_name: dengue virus NS5 RdRp–inhibitor ITC assay
_provenance.target_class: flavivirus RNA-dependent RNA polymerase binding thermodynamics
